title: Revendor everything
body:
First patch matches libpod patch by @vrothberg to start  using releases for Debian inclusion.
Second patch just vendors in the latest code.

comments.body: I saw that happen and could not figure out why.  Turns out gcc-go has older version of gofmt, and for some reason I was using it.  Remove the package and now gofmt fixes it correctly.

:pushpin: Commit 812f7e2 has been approved by `giuseppe`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=giuseppe 812f7e249131a3310994cf84c8bc3b69eee78c39 -->

Two Travis jobs keep failing with:
```
$ git checkout -qf 0322aef339757ddafbd252358038373b84ccdc64
fatal: reference is not a tree: 0322aef339757ddafbd252358038373b84ccdc64
The command "git checkout -qf 0322aef339757ddafbd252358038373b84ccdc64" failed and exited with 128 during .
```
@rhatdan, mind `git commit --amend` + force push to hopefully workaround the Travis hiccup? I restarted the two tests around five times over the past hour but they keep failing.

:pushpin: Commit d4262c1 has been approved by `giuseppe`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=giuseppe d4262c17ff884e7e105e3026b72618b0a5861b1a -->
:hourglass: Testing commit d4262c1 with merge a61daa1...
I restarted the two Travis tests. It's odd that alpine fails to be pulled that often.
@rhatdan @giuseppe I think it's quicker to restart the failing jobs individually. If we restart all, more can flake.

the "rh-atomic-bot retry" should restart only Homu and the merge.

We should probably move Buildah to the openshift-merge-robot, anyone knows how to do it?
Restarted the one failing test again: https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/builds/479834964?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/3eef1ed0bd12bcccf68f1ddfbf8387b3fdd3b0ae.1547551621752945408/index.html), [status-travis](https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/builds/479834964?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
Approved by: giuseppe
Pushing 3eef1ed0bd12bcccf68f1ddfbf8387b3fdd3b0ae to master...
